church.name: The Lutheran Church of the Savior

items.text: Let the peoples praise you, O God;
let all the peoples praise you!
Let the nations be glad and sing for joy,
for you judge the peoples with equity
and guide the nations upon earth.
Let the peoples praise you, O God;
let all the peoples praise you!
The earth has yielded its increase;
God, our God, shall bless us.
God shall bless us;
let all the ends of the earth fear him!
Glory be to the Father and to the Son
and to the Holy Spirit;
as it was in the beginning,
is now, and will be forever. Amen.
Let the peoples praise you, O God;
let all the peoples praise you!

sections.title: Communion Theology and Practice
sections.text:
Please see our statement of Communion Theology and Practice
on the back page of the worship bulletin.
If you are a visitor with us this morning and wish to receive the
Lord’s Supper, please speak with the pastor before the service.
Thank you.
Third Sunday after Pentecost
Divine Service

items.text: Stand

sections.title: Everlasting God
sections.stanzaPlan: Verse, Chorus 1, Verse, Chorus 2, Bridge, Chorus 2

items.text: The sign of the cross may be made by all in remembrance of their Baptism.

items.speaker: P
items.text: In the name of the Father and of the T Son and of the Holy Spirit.

items.speaker: C
items.text: Amen.

items.speaker: P
items.text: If we say we have no sin, we deceive ourselves, and the truth is not in us.

items.speaker: C
items.text: But if we confess our sins, God, who is faithful and just, will forgive our sins and cleanse us from all unrighteousness. Kneel/Stand Silence for reflection on God’s Word and for self-examination.

items.speaker: P
items.text: Let us then confess our sins to God our Father.

items.speaker: C
items.text: Most merciful God, we confess that we are by nature sinful and unclean. We have sinned against You in thought, word, and deed, by what we have done and by what we have left undone. We have not loved You with our whole heart; we have not loved our neighbors as ourselves. We justly deserve Your present and eternal punishment. For the sake of Your Son, Jesus Christ, have mercy on us. Forgive us, renew us, and lead us, so that we may delight in Your will and walk in Your ways to the glory of Your holy name. Amen.

items.speaker: P
items.text: Almighty God in His mercy has given His Son to die for you and for His sake forgives you all your sins. As a called and ordained servant of Christ, and by His authority, I therefore forgive you all your sins in the name of the Father and of the T Son and of the Holy Spirit.

items.speaker: C
items.text: Amen.
Stand

sections.title: Resound in Praise
sections.stanzaPlan: Verse, Chorus x2, Verse, Chorus x2

items.speaker: A
items.text: Together, we speak Psalm 100 responsively: 1 Make a joyful noise to the LORD, all the earth! 2 Serve the LORD with gladness! Come into his presence with singing! 3 Know that the LORD, he is God! It is he who made us, and we are his; we are his people, and the sheep of his pasture. 4 Enter his gates with thanksgiving, and his courts with praise! Give thanks to him; bless his name! 5 For the LORD is good; his steadfast love endures forever, and his faithfulness to all generations.

items.speaker: C
items.text: Glory be to the Father and to the Son and to the Holy Spirit; as it was in the beginning, is now, and will be forever. Amen.

sections.title: Salutation and Collect of the Day

items.speaker: P
items.text: The Lord be with you.

items.speaker: C
items.text: And also with you.

items.speaker: P
items.text: Let us pray. Almighty, eternal God, in the Word of Your apostles and prophets You have proclaimed to us Your saving will. Grant us faith to believe Your promises that we may receive eternal salvation; through Jesus Christ, our Lord, who lives and reigns with You and the Holy Spirit, one God, now and forever.

items.speaker: C
items.text: Amen.
Sit

items.speaker: A
items.text: This is the Word of the Lord.

items.speaker: C
items.text: Thanks be to God. 5

items.speaker: A
items.text: This is the Word of the Lord.

items.speaker: C
items.text: Thanks be to God.

items.speaker: P
items.text: The Holy Gospel according to St. Matthew, the ninth chapter.

items.speaker: C
items.text: Glory to You, O Lord. 35 Jesus went throughout all the cities and villages, teaching in their synagogues and proclaiming the gospel of the kingdom and healing 36 every disease and every affliction. When he saw the crowds, he had compassion for them, because they were harassed and helpless, like 37 sheep without a shepherd. Then he said to his disciples, “The harvest is 38 plentiful, but the laborers are few; therefore pray earnestly to the Lord of the harvest to send out laborers into his harvest.”

items.speaker: P
items.text: This is the Gospel of the Lord.

items.speaker: C
items.text: Praise to You, O Christ. 7

items.speaker: C
items.text: I believe in one God, the Father Almighty, maker of heaven and earth and of all things visible and invisible. And in one Lord Jesus Christ, the only-begotten Son of God, begotten of His Father before all worlds, God of God, Light of Light, very God of very God, begotten, not made, being of one substance with the Father, by whom all things were made; who for us men and for our salvation came down from heaven and was incarnate by the Holy Spirit of the virgin Mary and was made man; and was crucified also for us under Pontius Pilate. He suffered and was buried. And the third day He rose again according to the Scriptures and ascended into heaven and sits at the right hand of the Father. And He will come again with glory to judge both the living and the dead, whose kingdom will have no end. And I believe in the Holy Spirit, the Lord and giver of life, who proceeds from the Father and the Son, who with the Father and the Son together is worshiped and glorified, who spoke by the prophets. And I believe in one holy Christian and apostolic Church, I acknowledge one Baptism for the remission of sins, and I look for the resurrection of the dead and the life T of the world to come. Amen.
Sit

sections.title: Mighty To Save
sections.stanzaPlan: Verse 1, Chorus, Verse 2, Chorus, Bridge, Chorus x2

items.speaker: L
items.text: Let us pray for the whole Church of God in Christ Jesus and for all people according to their needs. The response after each petition is as follows:

items.speaker: L
items.text: Lord, in your mercy:

items.speaker: C
items.text: Hear our prayer.

items.speaker: C
items.text: Amen.
Lord’s Prayer

items.speaker: P
items.text: Lord, remember us in Your kingdom and teach us to pray:

items.speaker: C
items.text: Our Father who art in heaven, hallowed be Thy name, Thy kingdom come, Thy will be done on earth as it is in heaven; give us this day our daily bread; and forgive us our trespasses as we forgive those who trespass against us; and lead us not into temptation, but deliver us from evil. For Thine is the kingdom and the power and the glory forever and ever. Amen.

items.speaker: P
items.text: The grace of our Lord T Jesus Christ and the love of God and the communion of the Holy Spirit be with you all.

items.speaker: C
items.text: Amen.
Sit

sections.title: Resources for Meditation this Week
sections.text:
THIRD SUNDAY AFTER PENTECOST  14 June 2026
Readings
Exodus 19:2–8
Romans 5:6–15
Matthew 9:35—10:8 (9–20)
The Lord Our God Saves Us in Love and Cares for Us by the Ministry
of His Gospel
The holy Triune God “shows his love for us in that while we were still
sinners,” ungodly and at enmity with Him, “Christ died for us” (Rom. 5:8).
The incarnate Son has justified us by His blood and reconciled us to His
God and Father (Rom. 5:9–10). Whereas sin and death originated with
Adam, forgiveness and life abound for all his children through “that one
man Jesus Christ” (Rom. 5:12–17). As the Lord brought Israel out of Egypt,
so does He bring us to Himself by the Gospel and make of us “a kingdom
of priests and a holy nation” (Ex. 19:6) by our Baptism into Christ. For “all
that the LORD has spoken” (Ex. 19:8), Christ has done for us. As He has
gone up to God by His cross and resurrection, so does He bring us to the
Father in Himself (Ex. 19:3–4). Nor does He leave us “harassed and
helpless, like sheep without a shepherd” (Matt. 9:36), but He sends men
with authority “to heal every disease and every affliction” by His
forgiveness of sins (Matt. 10:1). In their proclamation, “the kingdom of
heaven is at hand” (Matt. 10:7).
FOURTH SUNDAY AFTER PENTECOST  21 June 2026
Readings
Jeremiah 20:7–13
Romans 6:12–23
Matthew 10:5a, 21–33
Delivered from Sin and Death, You Now Live before God in the
Righteousness of Christ
The outcome of sin is death, “but the free gift of God is eternal life in
Christ Jesus our Lord” (Rom. 6:23). He has set you free from the slavery of
sin and has brought you “from death to life” (Rom. 6:13). No longer are
you under the condemnation of the Law, but you live “under grace” (Rom.
6:14). Such is your courage in the face of “those who kill the body but
cannot kill the soul” (Matt. 10:28). For though “you will be hated by all”
and maligned by the world for the name of Christ (Matt. 10:22, 25), you
abide in the care of your Father in heaven, who numbers “even the hairs
of your head” and values you more “than many sparrows” (Matt. 10:30–
31). By the Word of Christ, you have become like Him, your Teacher and
Master in whom you endure to the end, and “will be saved” (Matt. 10:22,
25). For He is with you “as a dread warrior,” who has overcome your
enemies (Jer. 20:11). By the righteousness of faith, He delivers your heart,
mind, body and life “from the hand of evildoers,” and He brings you into
the land of the living (Jer. 20:12–13).

sections.title: Communion Theology and Practice
sections.text:
Welcome! In Holy Communion, our Lord invites us to eat and drink His
true Body and Blood for the forgiveness of sins and eternal life
(Matthew 26:26-28). In preparation for this sacrament, each
communicant should examine him or herself and come to the Lord’s
Table seeking these things. As the Small Catechism teaches, “a person
is truly worthy and well prepared who has faith in these words: ‘Given
and shed for you for the forgiveness of sins.’” We likewise take
seriously St. Paul's admonition in 1 Corinthians 11:27-30 regarding
recognizing the Lord's Body and Blood in this sacrament and receiving
it worthily. We also believe that Holy Communion is a profession of
confessional unity in which all who commune at the Lord's Table are
united in their confession and faith (1 Corinthians 11:26).
Therefore, we practice what is often called “closed communion,” in
which we invite those Christians who have been baptized and
instructed in the Christian faith, who share in the same confession of
faith as the Lutheran Church—Missouri Synod, and who believe that
Jesus offers here His true Body and Blood, in, with, and under the
elements of bread and wine, for the strengthening of their bodies and
souls, cleansing them of their old nature and giving them new life, to
join us at His table.
If you are new here, have not been baptized, or have not received
instruction with regard to the teachings of the Lutheran Church
regarding the Lord's Supper, have doubts about the nature of the
sacrament, or have questions, please introduce yourself to our pastor
before the service. He will be happy to discuss these things with you
and help determine whether or not you should commune. If you do
not wish to commune, have not been baptized, or have not been
instructed in the Christian faith, we invite you to come receive a
blessing. When you come to the altar, please come forward with your
arms crossed to indicate that you would like the pastor to bless you. If
you would like to learn more about the Christian faith or wish to be
baptized, please speak to our pastor!
